title: Wedelia cuyabensis
taxonomy: Wedelia cuyabensis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to Mexico and Central America, where it grows in a variety of habitats including forests and grasslands. The plant is classified in the following manner: it belongs to the genus Wedelia, tribe Heliantheae, and family Asteraceae. The scientific classification of Wedelia cuyabensis is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, subfamily Heliantheae, tribe Heliantheae, and genus Wedelia. Further research may provide more specific details about the classification of this plant.
uses: Wedelia cuyabensis, a species of flowering plant, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its leaves and flowers are rich in bioactive compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In traditional medicine, Wedelia cuyabensis is often used to treat digestive issues and to reduce inflammation. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Wedelia cuyabensis for medicinal purposes.
